I have enough information. Here is the analysis.

## Step 1 — Dispatch pipeline trace

| # | Method | file:line | What it does | What it calls next |
|---|--------|-----------|--------------|--------------------|
| 1 | `Flask.wsgi_app(environ, start_response)` | src/flask/app.py:1566 | WSGI entry; builds + pushes `RequestContext`, runs dispatch, returns the response as a WSGI iterable, pops ctx in `finally`. | `request_context(environ)` → `ctx.push()` → `self.full_dispatch_request(ctx)` (on error → `self.handle_exception(ctx, e)`); finally → `ctx.pop(error)` |
| 2 | `Flask.full_dispatch_request(ctx)` | src/flask/app.py:992 | Sends `request_started`, runs pre-processing + dispatch with user-exception handling, then finalizes. | `request_started.send(...)` → `self.preprocess_request(ctx)` → if `None`: `self.dispatch_request(ctx)` (on error → `self.handle_user_exception(ctx, e)`) → `self.finalize_request(ctx, rv)` |
| 3 | `Flask.preprocess_request(ctx)` | src/flask/app.py:1366 | Runs URL-value preprocessors and `before_request` funcs for app + blueprints (innermost first). Non-`None` return short-circuits the view. | iterates `url_value_preprocessors`, then `before_request_funcs` via `self.ensure_sync(...)` |
| 4 | `Flask.dispatch_request(ctx)` | src/flask/app.py:966 | Raises any routing exception, handles automatic OPTIONS, otherwise calls the matched view function with `view_args`. | `self.raise_routing_exception` / `self.make_default_options_response(ctx)` / `self.ensure_sync(self.view_functions[rule.endpoint])(**view_args)` |
| 5 | `Flask.finalize_request(ctx, rv, from_error_handler=False)` | src/flask/app.py:1021 | Converts the view return value into a response, runs after-request hooks, fires `request_finished`. Safe-mode for error handlers. | `self.make_response(rv)` → `self.process_response(ctx, response)` → `request_finished.send(...)` |
